metadata.title: AWS SysOps Administrator SOA-C02 Practice Questions

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to receive an email notification when CPU utilization on an EC2 instance exceeds 80% for 5 consecutive minutes. Which combination of AWS services should be used?
options: CloudWatch alarm with an SNS topic subscription
CloudTrail log with a Lambda function
AWS Config rule with an SES notification
Trusted Advisor check with an EventBridge rule
questions.explanation: CloudWatch alarms monitor metrics like CPU utilization and can trigger actions when thresholds are breached. An SNS topic with an email subscription delivers the notification. CloudTrail logs API calls, not metrics. AWS Config evaluates resource configurations, not performance metrics.

questions.question: A company wants to trigger an alarm only when BOTH CPU utilization exceeds 80% AND memory usage exceeds 70% on an EC2 instance simultaneously. What should the SysOps administrator configure?
options: A CloudWatch composite alarm combining two metric alarms
Two separate CloudWatch alarms with the same SNS topic
A single CloudWatch alarm with multiple metric math expressions
An EventBridge rule with two CloudWatch metric conditions
questions.explanation: CloudWatch composite alarms combine multiple metric alarms using AND/OR logic. This ensures the alarm only triggers when both conditions are true simultaneously. Two separate alarms would trigger independently, not in combination. Note that memory is a custom metric requiring the CloudWatch agent.

questions.question: An application running on EC2 instances behind an ALB experiences intermittent 5xx errors. The SysOps administrator needs to identify which specific backend instances are generating the errors. Which CloudWatch metric and dimension should be used?
options: HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count metric with the TargetGroup dimension
HTTPCode_ELB_5XX_Count metric with the LoadBalancer dimension
UnHealthyHostCount metric with the AvailabilityZone dimension
RequestCount metric with the TargetGroup dimension
questions.explanation: HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count tracks 5xx errors generated by backend targets (EC2 instances), while HTTPCode_ELB_5XX_Count tracks errors generated by the load balancer itself. The TargetGroup dimension helps identify which target group (and its registered instances) is producing the errors. UnHealthyHostCount only shows health check failures, not HTTP errors.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to monitor API calls made to AWS services across all regions in an AWS account. Which service should be enabled?
options: AWS CloudTrail with a multi-region trail
Amazon CloudWatch Logs with cross-region replication
AWS Config with multi-region aggregation
VPC Flow Logs with cross-region delivery
questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records API calls made to AWS services. A multi-region trail captures events from all AWS regions and delivers them to a single S3 bucket. CloudWatch Logs stores log data but does not inherently track API calls. AWS Config tracks resource configurations, not API activity.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to find all EC2 instances that were terminated in the past 7 days, including who terminated them. Which approach is MOST efficient?
options: Query CloudTrail event history filtering for TerminateInstances events
Search CloudWatch Logs for EC2 termination log entries
Use AWS Config to view configuration timeline of terminated instances
Check the EC2 console's recent activity dashboard
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records all API calls including TerminateInstances events with the identity of the caller (IAM user/role), timestamp, and source IP. CloudTrail event history provides 90 days of management events that can be filtered by event name. AWS Config tracks configuration changes but does not capture who made the API call.

questions.question: A company requires that all CloudTrail log files are validated for integrity to detect any tampering. The logs must also be searchable within 5 minutes of the API call occurring. What configuration achieves both requirements?
options: Enable CloudTrail log file validation and configure CloudTrail to deliver logs to CloudWatch Logs
Enable CloudTrail log file validation and enable S3 server access logging on the trail bucket
Enable CloudTrail Insights and configure an S3 event notification to trigger Lambda
Enable CloudTrail log file validation and configure S3 Object Lock on the trail bucket
questions.explanation: CloudTrail log file validation creates a digest file with hashes that can be used to verify log integrity. Delivering logs to CloudWatch Logs enables near real-time searching via Logs Insights (typically within 5 minutes). S3 Object Lock prevents deletion but does not enable searching. CloudTrail Insights detects unusual API activity patterns but does not provide searchability.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator wants to create a centralized view of key metrics from multiple AWS accounts and regions in a single dashboard. Which service provides this capability?
options: Amazon CloudWatch cross-account dashboards
AWS Systems Manager OpsCenter
AWS Config aggregator
Amazon Managed Grafana
questions.explanation: CloudWatch supports cross-account and cross-region dashboards that allow you to create a single view of metrics from multiple accounts and regions. While Amazon Managed Grafana can also provide cross-account dashboards, CloudWatch dashboards are the native AWS solution specifically designed for this purpose.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to query application logs stored in CloudWatch Logs to find the top 10 most frequent error messages in the past 24 hours. Which tool should be used?
options: CloudWatch Logs Insights with a stats query
CloudWatch metric filters with a top-N aggregation
Athena with a query on exported CloudWatch Logs in S3
CloudWatch Contributor Insights with a rule for error patterns
questions.explanation: CloudWatch Logs Insights allows ad-hoc querying of log data with SQL-like syntax including aggregation functions like stats count(*) by errorMessage | sort count desc | limit 10. Metric filters create numeric metrics from log patterns but do not support ad-hoc top-N queries. Athena could work but requires exporting logs to S3 first, adding latency.

questions.question: An EC2 instance is reporting high CPU utilization in CloudWatch, but the application team says the instance is idle. The SysOps administrator discovers the CloudWatch agent is not installed. What is the MOST likely explanation?
options: The default CloudWatch metrics report steal time from the hypervisor as CPU utilization
A background process or malware is consuming CPU that the application team is not aware of
CloudWatch basic monitoring samples CPU every 5 minutes, causing inaccurate readings
The instance type has burstable CPU credits that are being consumed by T-series baseline
questions.explanation: If CloudWatch reports high CPU but the application appears idle, a background process, system process, or potentially malware could be consuming CPU resources. The default EC2 CloudWatch metrics accurately report total CPU utilization at the hypervisor level. Installing the CloudWatch agent would provide OS-level metrics to identify which processes are consuming CPU.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to automatically remediate EC2 instances that fail status checks. The remediation should recover the instance while preserving the instance ID, private IP, Elastic IP, and instance metadata. What is the BEST approach?
options: Create a CloudWatch alarm on StatusCheckFailed_System with an EC2 recover action
Create a CloudWatch alarm that triggers a Lambda function to stop and start the instance
Configure an Auto Scaling group with a health check that replaces unhealthy instances
Use Systems Manager Automation to run the AWS-RestartEC2Instance runbook
questions.explanation: The EC2 recover action on a CloudWatch alarm preserves the instance ID, private IP, Elastic IP, instance metadata, and placement group. It migrates the instance to new hardware. Stop/start also migrates hardware but changes the public IP (unless Elastic IP). Auto Scaling replacement creates a new instance with a new ID. The recover action specifically handles system status check failures.

questions.question: Which CloudWatch feature allows a SysOps administrator to create a custom metric from log data by counting the number of times a specific error string appears?
options: Metric filters
Logs Insights queries
CloudWatch Alarms
CloudWatch Anomaly Detection
questions.explanation: CloudWatch metric filters extract metric observations from log data by matching patterns. For example, a filter can count occurrences of 'ERROR' in a log group and publish it as a custom metric. Logs Insights performs ad-hoc queries but does not create persistent metrics. Alarms act on existing metrics, they do not create them.

questions.question: A company uses AWS Config to evaluate compliance of its resources. A new rule requires that all EBS volumes must be encrypted. The administrator needs to identify existing non-compliant volumes and prevent future non-compliant ones. What should be configured?
options: An AWS Config managed rule for encrypted-volumes with automatic remediation using SSM Automation
A CloudTrail event rule that triggers Lambda to encrypt volumes on creation
An IAM policy that denies ec2:CreateVolume without encryption parameters
A custom Config rule using Lambda that encrypts non-compliant volumes directly
questions.explanation: AWS Config managed rule 'encrypted-volumes' evaluates whether EBS volumes are encrypted. Combined with automatic remediation using SSM Automation, it can identify non-compliant resources and take corrective action. An IAM policy with a condition key (ec2:Encrypted) could prevent future unencrypted volumes but would not identify existing ones. AWS Config provides both detection and remediation.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to set up an automated workflow that: (1) detects when an IAM access key has not been rotated in 90 days, (2) sends a notification to the key owner, and (3) disables the key if not rotated within 7 days of notification. Which architecture achieves this with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: AWS Config rule for access-keys-rotated triggering EventBridge to invoke a Step Functions workflow
CloudWatch Events scheduled rule triggering Lambda to check IAM credential reports daily
IAM Access Analyzer findings triggering SNS notifications with a separate Lambda for key rotation
Trusted Advisor check for IAM key rotation with CloudWatch alarm triggering Lambda
questions.explanation: AWS Config rule 'access-keys-rotated' continuously evaluates IAM access key age. When non-compliant, EventBridge captures the Config compliance change event and triggers a Step Functions workflow that orchestrates the notification and delayed disabling logic. Step Functions provides built-in wait states and retry logic with minimal custom code. A standalone Lambda approach requires managing state and scheduling separately.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator notices that detailed monitoring is not enabled on several EC2 instances. What is the difference between basic and detailed monitoring in CloudWatch?
options: Basic monitoring collects metrics every 5 minutes; detailed monitoring collects every 1 minute
Basic monitoring collects CPU only; detailed monitoring collects CPU, memory, and disk
Basic monitoring is free for 7 days; detailed monitoring retains data for 15 months
Basic monitoring uses the CloudWatch agent; detailed monitoring uses the hypervisor
questions.explanation: Basic monitoring sends metrics to CloudWatch every 5 minutes at no additional cost. Detailed monitoring sends metrics every 1 minute for an additional charge. Both use the same hypervisor-level metrics (CPU, network, disk I/O, status checks). Memory and disk space metrics require the CloudWatch agent regardless of monitoring level.

questions.question: An EventBridge rule needs to capture all S3 bucket policy changes and trigger a Lambda function for compliance review. Which event source and detail type should be configured?
options: Source: aws.s3, detail-type: AWS API Call via CloudTrail, with detail filtering for PutBucketPolicy
Source: aws.s3, detail-type: S3 Bucket Policy Change
Source: aws.config, detail-type: Config Rules Compliance Change
Source: aws.cloudtrail, detail-type: CloudTrail API Call
questions.explanation: EventBridge receives S3 API calls via CloudTrail integration. The correct pattern uses source 'aws.s3' with detail-type 'AWS API Call via CloudTrail' and filters on the specific API call (PutBucketPolicy) in the detail section. CloudTrail must be enabled for this to work. There is no native 'S3 Bucket Policy Change' event type.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to detect anomalous API call patterns that might indicate a compromised IAM credential. The solution should automatically identify unusual activity without manually defining thresholds. Which feature should be configured?
options: CloudTrail Insights events
CloudWatch Anomaly Detection on CloudTrail metrics
GuardDuty with CloudTrail data source enabled
AWS Config custom rule with Lambda-based analysis
questions.explanation: Amazon GuardDuty analyzes CloudTrail management events, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs using machine learning to detect anomalous activity such as unusual API calls from compromised credentials, cryptocurrency mining, and unauthorized infrastructure deployments. CloudTrail Insights detects unusual API volume but is less comprehensive for security threats. GuardDuty is specifically designed for threat detection.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to collect memory utilization and disk space metrics from Linux EC2 instances. Which approach is correct?
options: Install and configure the CloudWatch agent with a configuration file specifying mem and disk metrics
Enable detailed monitoring on the EC2 instances to include OS-level metrics
Create custom metrics using the AWS CLI put-metric-data command in a cron job
Enable EC2 instance connect and use Systems Manager to push metrics
questions.explanation: The CloudWatch agent must be installed on EC2 instances to collect OS-level metrics like memory utilization and disk space. The agent configuration file specifies which metrics to collect. Default EC2 metrics (basic or detailed) only include hypervisor-level metrics like CPU, network, and disk I/O. While custom metrics via put-metric-data could work, the CloudWatch agent is the recommended and most efficient approach.

questions.question: A company wants to receive a notification when their estimated AWS monthly charges exceed $500. Which service should the SysOps administrator configure?
options: CloudWatch billing alarm with an SNS notification
AWS Budgets with an email alert
Cost Explorer with a scheduled report
Trusted Advisor cost optimization check
questions.explanation: A CloudWatch billing alarm monitors the EstimatedCharges metric and triggers when it exceeds the specified threshold. An SNS topic with email subscription delivers the notification. AWS Budgets is also valid for this purpose and offers more features like forecasted alerts, but a CloudWatch billing alarm is the simplest approach for a single threshold notification.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to centralize CloudTrail logs from 15 AWS accounts in an organization into a single S3 bucket in a dedicated logging account. The logs must be encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key. What is the correct configuration?
options: Create an organization trail from the management account, specify the logging account's S3 bucket, and update the KMS key policy to allow cloudtrail.amazonaws.com and all member accounts
Create individual trails in each account pointing to the logging account's S3 bucket with cross-account S3 bucket policies
Enable CloudTrail Lake in the management account and configure ingestion from all member accounts
Use AWS Control Tower to automatically configure trails with centralized logging
questions.explanation: An organization trail created from the management account automatically logs events from all accounts in the organization. The S3 bucket policy must allow CloudTrail to write from the organization, and the KMS key policy must grant encrypt permissions to cloudtrail.amazonaws.com and decrypt permissions to accounts that need to read the logs. Individual trails per account add unnecessary management overhead.

questions.question: An Auto Scaling group has a CloudWatch alarm configured to scale out when average CPU exceeds 70%. During a traffic spike, the alarm triggers but new instances take 10 minutes to fully initialize. During this time, the alarm triggers additional scale-out actions. How should the administrator prevent excessive scaling?
options: Configure a scaling cooldown period of at least 10 minutes
Change the alarm evaluation period to 15 minutes
Use step scaling instead of simple scaling
Increase the instance warmup time in the scaling policy
questions.explanation: A scaling cooldown period prevents the Auto Scaling group from launching additional instances until the cooldown expires, allowing recently launched instances to stabilize and handle load. The default cooldown is 300 seconds. Setting it to match the initialization time prevents over-scaling. Step scaling with instance warmup is also valid, but cooldown is the most direct solution for simple scaling policies.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to view the compliance status of AWS Config rules across multiple accounts and regions from a single dashboard. Which feature should be used?
options: AWS Config aggregator
CloudWatch cross-account dashboard
AWS Organizations consolidated view
AWS Security Hub multi-account findings
questions.explanation: An AWS Config aggregator collects configuration and compliance data from multiple accounts and regions, providing a centralized view. You can create an aggregator in a central account and authorize source accounts. While Security Hub also aggregates findings, AWS Config aggregator is specifically designed for Config rule compliance data.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to detect when an EC2 security group is modified to allow inbound SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0. The detection must be near real-time. Which solution requires the LEAST effort?
options: AWS Config managed rule restricted-ssh with auto-remediation
EventBridge rule matching AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress API calls via CloudTrail
VPC Flow Logs analyzed by a Lambda function for SSH traffic patterns
GuardDuty finding for unauthorized access with SNS notification
questions.explanation: AWS Config managed rule 'restricted-ssh' specifically checks whether security groups allow unrestricted SSH access (0.0.0.0/0 on port 22). It evaluates on every configuration change and can trigger automatic remediation to remove the offending rule. This is a turnkey solution with no custom code. EventBridge with CloudTrail would work but requires more configuration to parse the specific ingress rule details.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator is troubleshooting an application that intermittently returns errors. Application logs are in CloudWatch Logs. The administrator needs to correlate errors with deployment events and infrastructure changes. Which approach provides the BEST visibility?
options: Create a CloudWatch dashboard combining Logs Insights query widgets, metric widgets for deployment markers, and AWS Config configuration timeline data
Export all logs to S3 and use Athena for correlation queries
Use X-Ray to trace requests and correlate with CloudTrail events
Configure CloudWatch Contributor Insights to identify top error contributors
questions.explanation: A CloudWatch dashboard can combine multiple data sources: Logs Insights widgets for log analysis, metric widgets showing deployment annotations and infrastructure metrics, and embed links to Config timeline data. This provides a single-pane view correlating errors with changes. X-Ray is for distributed tracing, not log-infrastructure correlation. Athena requires data export and is not real-time.

questions.question: AWS Trusted Advisor has flagged several EC2 instances as underutilized (CPU below 10% for 14 days). What is the recommended first remediation step?
options: Right-size the instances to a smaller instance type based on actual utilization patterns
Immediately terminate all underutilized instances to save costs
Convert all flagged instances to Spot Instances
Enable Auto Scaling to automatically adjust instance sizes
questions.explanation: Right-sizing involves changing to a smaller instance type that matches actual usage. This is the recommended approach for underutilized instances. Terminating immediately may disrupt workloads. Spot Instances are for fault-tolerant workloads and may not be appropriate. Auto Scaling adjusts the number of instances, not individual instance sizes. Always validate utilization data before making changes.

questions.question: Which CloudWatch metric should a SysOps administrator monitor to detect when an SQS queue has a growing backlog of unprocessed messages?
options: ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible
NumberOfMessagesSent
NumberOfMessagesReceived
ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage
questions.explanation: ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible shows the number of messages available for retrieval from the queue. A growing number indicates consumers are not processing messages fast enough. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage is also useful as it indicates how long messages have been waiting, but NumberOfMessagesVisible is the primary backlog indicator.

questions.question: A company has configured CloudWatch alarms for all production EC2 instances. During a large-scale event, the operations team receives hundreds of individual alarm notifications, making it difficult to identify the root cause. How should the administrator redesign the alerting strategy?
options: Implement composite alarms that group related alarms and suppress child alarm actions when the parent triggers
Increase alarm evaluation periods to reduce notification frequency
Route all alarms to a single SNS topic with a Lambda function that aggregates notifications
Disable individual instance alarms and create aggregate metric alarms only
questions.explanation: Composite alarms combine multiple alarms using Boolean logic and can suppress child alarm notifications when the parent composite alarm triggers. This reduces notification noise during widespread events while maintaining individual alarm visibility. The ActionsSuppressor feature specifically prevents cascading notifications. Increasing evaluation periods just delays notifications without solving the noise problem.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to automatically start an EC2 instance every weekday at 8 AM and stop it at 6 PM to save costs. Which approach is MOST operationally efficient?
options: Create EventBridge scheduled rules that trigger Systems Manager Automation documents to start and stop the instance
Write a Lambda function with two CloudWatch Events cron rules
Use AWS Instance Scheduler solution from the Solutions Library
Configure the instance's user data script to run a shutdown cron job
questions.explanation: EventBridge scheduled rules with Systems Manager Automation documents (AWS-StartEC2Instance and AWS-StopEC2Instance) provide a fully managed, serverless solution without custom code. While Lambda with CloudWatch Events also works, SSM Automation is preferred because it requires no code and handles API calls natively. AWS Instance Scheduler is a third-party solution that adds complexity.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to publish a custom CloudWatch metric for the number of active user sessions tracked by an application. The metric should have dimensions for environment (prod/dev) and region. What is the correct approach?
options: Use the CloudWatch PutMetricData API with namespace, metric name, value, and dimension parameters
Configure a CloudWatch metric filter on application logs to extract the session count
Install the CloudWatch agent and configure a custom metric in the agent configuration
Create a CloudWatch dashboard with a custom widget that queries the application API
questions.explanation: The PutMetricData API allows publishing custom metrics with custom namespaces, metric names, values, units, and dimensions. The application can call this API directly or through the AWS SDK. Metric filters extract metrics from log data but cannot directly access application state. The CloudWatch agent collects OS-level metrics, not application-specific custom metrics.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator wants to visualize the relationship between EC2 CPU utilization and the number of requests hitting an ALB on the same time axis. Which CloudWatch dashboard widget should be used?
options: A single line widget with both metrics plotted on the same graph
Two separate number widgets showing current values
A stacked area widget combining both metrics
An alarm status widget for both metrics
questions.explanation: A line widget can display multiple metrics from different namespaces on the same time-series graph, making it easy to visually correlate patterns. This allows the administrator to see if CPU spikes correspond with request count increases. Number widgets show point-in-time values without trends. Alarm status widgets only show alarm states, not metric values.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator discovers that CloudWatch Logs for an application log group are consuming significant storage. The administrator needs to reduce costs while retaining the ability to analyze logs from the past 30 days. Logs older than 30 days must be archived for compliance. What is the BEST approach?
options: Set the log group retention policy to 30 days and create a subscription filter to export logs to S3 with lifecycle policies for archiving
Set the log group retention policy to never expire and use Logs Insights to query only recent data
Export all logs to S3 daily using CreateExportTask and delete the CloudWatch log group
Compress log entries before sending them to CloudWatch Logs
questions.explanation: Setting retention to 30 days automatically deletes logs older than 30 days from CloudWatch Logs, reducing storage costs. A subscription filter streams logs in near real-time to S3 (via Kinesis Firehose) where lifecycle policies can transition them to cheaper storage classes (Glacier) for long-term compliance archiving. This provides both cost optimization and compliance retention.

questions.question: A company needs to ensure that its RDS MySQL database remains available if the primary Availability Zone experiences an outage. Which configuration provides automatic failover?
options: RDS Multi-AZ deployment
RDS read replica in another AZ
RDS automated backups with point-in-time recovery
RDS cross-region read replica
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ deployment maintains a synchronous standby replica in a different Availability Zone. If the primary AZ fails, RDS automatically fails over to the standby (typically within 60-120 seconds). Read replicas use asynchronous replication and do not provide automatic failover. Automated backups enable recovery but require manual restoration.

questions.question: An Auto Scaling group spans three Availability Zones with a minimum of 6 instances. Currently there are 2 instances in each AZ. If one AZ experiences a complete failure, what does Auto Scaling do?
options: Launches 2 new instances distributed across the remaining 2 AZs to maintain 6 total instances
Launches 2 new instances in one of the remaining AZs only
Reduces the desired capacity to 4 to match available AZs
Waits for the failed AZ to recover before taking action
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling detects the AZ failure and replaces unhealthy instances. It distributes new instances across the remaining healthy AZs to maintain the minimum capacity of 6 instances. Auto Scaling uses AZ rebalancing to keep instances as evenly distributed as possible. It does not reduce desired capacity due to an AZ failure.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to create a backup strategy for an Amazon EFS file system. Which AWS service provides automated, policy-based backups for EFS?
options: AWS Backup
EFS lifecycle management
S3 Cross-Region Replication
DataSync scheduled transfers
questions.explanation: AWS Backup supports Amazon EFS and provides automated, policy-based backup scheduling with retention management. Backup plans can define frequency, retention period, and lifecycle rules (transition to cold storage). EFS lifecycle management moves infrequently accessed files between storage classes but does not create backups. DataSync transfers data but is not a backup service.

questions.question: A company has an RPO of 1 hour and an RTO of 15 minutes for its production database. Which RDS backup and recovery strategy meets these requirements?
options: Multi-AZ deployment with automated backups and transaction log backups every 5 minutes
Single-AZ with daily automated snapshots and manual snapshots before deployments
Read replica in another region with manual promotion
Multi-AZ with manual snapshots taken every hour
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ with automated backups provides continuous transaction log backups (every 5 minutes), achieving an RPO well under 1 hour via point-in-time recovery. Multi-AZ automatic failover completes in 60-120 seconds, meeting the 15-minute RTO. Daily snapshots provide only 24-hour RPO. Manual snapshots require human intervention, increasing RTO.

questions.question: A company requires a disaster recovery solution for a web application that provides an RTO of under 1 minute and RPO of near zero. The application uses an ALB, EC2 Auto Scaling, and RDS. Which DR strategy should the SysOps administrator implement?
options: Multi-site active/active with Route 53 health checks and Aurora Global Database
Warm standby with Route 53 failover and RDS cross-region read replica
Pilot light with minimal infrastructure in the DR region and RDS snapshots
Backup and restore with automated AMI copying and RDS automated backups
questions.explanation: Multi-site active/active runs full production infrastructure in multiple regions simultaneously. Route 53 health checks automatically route traffic away from an unhealthy region (sub-minute RTO). Aurora Global Database provides cross-region replication with typically under 1 second of lag (near-zero RPO). Warm standby requires scaling up, increasing RTO. Pilot light requires provisioning resources, increasing both RTO and RPO.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to configure Route 53 to automatically direct traffic away from an unhealthy primary endpoint to a backup endpoint. Which routing policy should be used?
options: Failover routing policy with health checks
Weighted routing policy with equal weights
Latency-based routing policy
Simple routing policy with multiple values
questions.explanation: Route 53 failover routing policy directs traffic to a primary resource when healthy and automatically routes to a secondary (standby) resource when the primary fails its health check. Weighted routing distributes traffic based on weights but does not provide automatic failover. Latency routing directs to the lowest-latency endpoint regardless of health.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to protect an S3 bucket containing critical data from accidental deletion. Objects must be recoverable even if someone deletes them. Which combination of features should be enabled?
options: S3 versioning and MFA Delete
S3 Object Lock in compliance mode
S3 Cross-Region Replication
S3 lifecycle policy with transition to Glacier
questions.explanation: S3 versioning preserves all versions of objects, so deleted objects can be recovered by removing the delete marker. MFA Delete requires multi-factor authentication to permanently delete object versions or disable versioning, preventing accidental or malicious permanent deletion. Object Lock prevents deletion but is typically used for regulatory compliance rather than accidental deletion protection.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator is designing a highly available architecture for a stateful application. The application stores session data locally on the EC2 instance. When instances are replaced by Auto Scaling, users lose their sessions. How should this be resolved?
options: Store session data in Amazon ElastiCache Redis and configure the application to use it as an external session store
Enable sticky sessions on the ALB to route users to the same instance
Use EBS volumes attached to each instance and copy data between instances
Increase the health check grace period to prevent premature instance replacement
questions.explanation: Externalizing session data to ElastiCache Redis makes the application stateless from the EC2 perspective. Any instance can serve any user because session data is stored externally. This allows Auto Scaling to freely replace instances without session loss. Sticky sessions only work while the original instance is alive. EBS volumes are tied to single instances and AZs.

questions.question: A company uses an ALB with target groups for its application. The health check is configured to use HTTP on port 80 with path /health. Instances are being marked unhealthy even though the application is running. What is the MOST likely cause?
options: The application's /health endpoint is returning a non-2xx status code
The security group does not allow inbound traffic on port 443
The instance is in a private subnet without internet access
The ALB listener is configured for HTTPS but the health check uses HTTP
questions.explanation: ALB health checks expect a 200 status code by default (configurable range 200-499). If the /health endpoint returns a 3xx redirect, 4xx error, or 5xx error, the target is marked unhealthy. Common causes include the application not listening on port 80, the /health path not existing (404), or the application returning an error. The ALB health check connects directly to the target, so it uses the health check port and protocol, not the listener port.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to create a consistent, point-in-time backup of multiple EBS volumes attached to a single EC2 instance. Which approach ensures data consistency across all volumes?
options: Create an EBS-backed AMI of the instance, which creates snapshots of all attached volumes atomically
Create individual EBS snapshots of each volume simultaneously using the AWS CLI
Stop the instance, create snapshots of all volumes, then start the instance
Use AWS Backup to create a backup of the instance
questions.explanation: Creating an AMI from a running instance triggers crash-consistent snapshots of all attached EBS volumes at the same point in time. This ensures cross-volume consistency. Individual snapshots may not be perfectly synchronized. Stopping the instance ensures consistency but causes downtime. AWS Backup also creates consistent snapshots but an AMI additionally captures the instance configuration.

questions.question: An application uses an Auto Scaling group with an ALB. During a deployment, new instances pass the EC2 status checks but fail the ALB health checks. Users receive 502 errors. What should the administrator investigate?
options: The application takes longer to start than the ALB health check grace period allows
The Auto Scaling group is using the wrong launch template version
The NAT gateway in the private subnet has reached its bandwidth limit
The ALB listener rule is routing traffic to the wrong target group
questions.explanation: If instances pass EC2 status checks (hardware/OS healthy) but fail ALB health checks (application not responding correctly), the application likely has not finished starting up. The health check grace period should be set long enough for the application to fully initialize. If it is too short, Auto Scaling considers the instance unhealthy and terminates it, creating a loop of launch-terminate cycles.

questions.question: A multi-tier application has a web tier in a public subnet and a database tier in a private subnet. The database tier must be highly available across AZs. During an AZ failure, the database must automatically become available in another AZ with no data loss. Which architecture meets these requirements?
options: RDS Multi-AZ with synchronous replication to a standby in another AZ
RDS with automated backups and point-in-time recovery
DynamoDB with global tables across AZs
EC2-hosted database with EBS Multi-Attach in two AZs
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ uses synchronous replication to maintain an identical standby in a different AZ. If the primary AZ fails, automatic failover promotes the standby with zero data loss (synchronous replication ensures all committed transactions are replicated). Point-in-time recovery has data loss up to the last backup interval. DynamoDB Global Tables are cross-region, not cross-AZ. EBS Multi-Attach is for within-AZ, not across AZs.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to implement a backup strategy for a DynamoDB table that supports point-in-time recovery to any second in the past 35 days. Which feature should be enabled?
options: DynamoDB Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR)
DynamoDB on-demand backup
AWS Backup with a DynamoDB backup plan
DynamoDB Streams with a Lambda archiver
questions.explanation: DynamoDB PITR provides continuous backups and allows restoration to any point in time within the last 35 days, with per-second granularity. On-demand backups create full snapshots at a specific point in time but do not support per-second recovery. AWS Backup can manage DynamoDB backups but PITR provides the granularity specified. DynamoDB Streams capture changes but require custom code to rebuild state.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure that an EC2 Auto Scaling group replaces instances that fail ELB health checks. What must be configured on the Auto Scaling group?
options: Set the health check type to ELB instead of EC2
Enable detailed monitoring on all instances
Configure a CloudWatch alarm for UnHealthyHostCount
Attach a lifecycle hook for instance termination
questions.explanation: By default, Auto Scaling groups use EC2 status checks for health evaluation. To also consider ELB health checks, the health check type must be set to ELB. When set to ELB, an instance is marked unhealthy if it fails either EC2 status checks or ELB health checks, and Auto Scaling replaces it. Without this setting, instances that fail ELB health checks but pass EC2 status checks remain in the group.

questions.question: A company needs to implement a pilot light DR strategy for a critical application. The primary region is us-east-1 and the DR region is us-west-2. The application uses RDS PostgreSQL, EC2 instances, and S3 for static assets. What components should be pre-provisioned in the DR region?
options: RDS cross-region read replica, AMIs copied to us-west-2, and S3 Cross-Region Replication enabled
Full copy of all EC2 instances in stopped state, RDS snapshot copied daily, and S3 lifecycle policy
CloudFormation stack ready to deploy, daily RDS snapshots in us-west-2, and S3 versioning
Route 53 health checks, Global Accelerator endpoint, and DynamoDB Global Tables
questions.explanation: A pilot light strategy pre-provisions the minimum core infrastructure needed for quick recovery. An RDS cross-region read replica provides near real-time data replication and can be promoted to primary quickly. AMIs in the DR region allow rapid EC2 deployment. S3 CRR keeps static assets synchronized. During failover, the read replica is promoted, EC2 instances are launched from AMIs, and Route 53 routes traffic to the DR region.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator notices that an Auto Scaling group is oscillating between scaling out and scaling in every few minutes, creating instability. What is the MOST likely cause and solution?
options: The scaling threshold is too close to current utilization; add a gap between scale-out and scale-in thresholds
The minimum and maximum capacity settings are incorrect
The launch template specifies an instance type with insufficient capacity
The CloudWatch alarm evaluation period is too long
questions.explanation: Oscillation (thrashing) occurs when the scale-out threshold is close to the scale-in threshold. After scaling out, the added capacity drops utilization below the scale-in threshold, triggering scale-in. Removing instances increases utilization above the scale-out threshold again. The solution is to create a gap (dead band) between the thresholds, for example scale out at 70% and scale in at 40%.

questions.question: Which AWS service allows a SysOps administrator to create centralized backup policies that automatically protect resources across multiple AWS accounts in an organization?
options: AWS Backup with organization-wide backup policies
AWS Systems Manager State Manager
Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager
AWS CloudFormation StackSets
questions.explanation: AWS Backup supports organization-wide backup policies that can be applied across accounts in AWS Organizations. Backup policies define backup plans, vault assignments, and resource selections that are automatically enforced in member accounts. Data Lifecycle Manager handles EBS snapshot and AMI lifecycle but is limited to a single account.

questions.question: A Route 53 failover configuration is not routing traffic to the secondary endpoint even though the primary endpoint is down. Health checks show the primary as healthy. What is the MOST likely issue?
options: The Route 53 health check is not configured to check the correct port or path of the primary endpoint
The secondary endpoint does not have a health check configured
The TTL on the DNS record is set too high
The Route 53 hosted zone is not associated with the correct VPC
questions.explanation: If the health check shows the primary as healthy despite being down, the health check is likely misconfigured. Common issues include checking the wrong port, the wrong path (e.g., / instead of /health), or checking a load balancer that returns 200 even when backend instances are down. The health check must accurately reflect the application's availability. Always verify the health check endpoint matches the actual application health.

questions.question: A company has a strict requirement that no more than one instance in an Auto Scaling group can be unavailable during instance refresh operations. The group has 4 instances across 2 AZs. Which instance refresh configuration satisfies this requirement?
options: Set MaxHealthyPercentage to 125% and MinHealthyPercentage to 75%
Set MaxHealthyPercentage to 100% and MinHealthyPercentage to 100%
Set MaxHealthyPercentage to 200% and MinHealthyPercentage to 50%
Set MaxHealthyPercentage to 150% and MinHealthyPercentage to 100%
questions.explanation: With 4 instances, MinHealthyPercentage of 75% means at least 3 instances (75% of 4 = 3) must be healthy at all times, ensuring no more than 1 is unavailable. MaxHealthyPercentage of 125% allows launching up to 5 instances (125% of 4 = 5) temporarily during the refresh. This enables launching a replacement before terminating the old instance, reducing the blast radius of the refresh operation.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to automate the creation and retention of EBS snapshots on a schedule. Snapshots older than 30 days should be automatically deleted. Which service should be used?
options: Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager (DLM)
AWS Backup
CloudWatch Events with Lambda
Systems Manager Automation
questions.explanation: Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager automates the creation, retention, and deletion of EBS snapshots based on policies. You can define the schedule, retention rules (by count or age), and tag-based targeting. AWS Backup also provides this capability but DLM is the purpose-built service for EBS snapshot lifecycle management with simpler configuration.

questions.question: During a planned maintenance window, a SysOps administrator needs to update the AMI used by an Auto Scaling group with zero downtime. What is the recommended approach?
options: Create a new launch template version with the updated AMI and perform an instance refresh
Modify the existing launch template to reference the new AMI ID
Terminate all existing instances and let Auto Scaling launch new ones with the updated AMI
Detach instances from the Auto Scaling group, update the AMI, and reattach
questions.explanation: Creating a new launch template version and performing an instance refresh is the recommended zero-downtime approach. Instance refresh gradually replaces instances by launching new ones with the updated AMI and terminating old ones, respecting the MinHealthyPercentage. You cannot detach instances and reattach with a different AMI. Terminating all instances causes complete downtime.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator manages an Aurora MySQL cluster that serves a global application. The company requires that the database can serve read traffic in Europe with low latency and can be promoted to primary in the EU region within 1 minute if the US region fails. Which feature should be used?
options: Aurora Global Database with a secondary cluster in the EU region
Aurora read replicas deployed in the EU region
RDS cross-region read replica with manual promotion
Aurora Serverless v2 with multi-region configuration
questions.explanation: Aurora Global Database replicates data from the primary region to up to five secondary regions with typical latency under 1 second. Each secondary region has a full copy of the data that can serve read traffic locally. During failover, a secondary cluster can be promoted to primary in typically under 1 minute. Standard Aurora read replicas are within a single region. RDS cross-region replicas have higher replication lag and slower promotion.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure that all EC2 instances, RDS databases, and EFS file systems in a production account are backed up daily with a 30-day retention period. The solution must be centrally managed. What is the MOST efficient approach?
options: Create an AWS Backup plan with a daily rule, 30-day retention, and resource assignment by tag
Create separate DLM policies for EBS snapshots, RDS automated backups, and EFS lifecycle policies
Write a Lambda function triggered by CloudWatch Events that creates backups for each service
Use CloudFormation to deploy backup resources for each service individually
questions.explanation: AWS Backup provides a centralized, unified backup solution that supports EC2, RDS, EFS, and many other AWS services. A single backup plan with resource assignment by tag (e.g., Backup: true) applies the policy to all matching resources regardless of service type. This is more efficient than managing separate backup mechanisms for each service. AWS Backup also provides centralized monitoring and reporting.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator configures an ALB health check with an interval of 30 seconds, a healthy threshold of 3, and an unhealthy threshold of 2. How long will it take for a healthy instance that starts failing health checks to be marked unhealthy?
options: 60 seconds (2 consecutive failed checks at 30-second intervals)
90 seconds (3 consecutive failed checks at 30-second intervals)
30 seconds (1 failed check)
150 seconds (5 consecutive failed checks at 30-second intervals)
questions.explanation: With an unhealthy threshold of 2 and an interval of 30 seconds, it takes 2 consecutive failed health checks to mark the target unhealthy: 2 x 30 seconds = 60 seconds. The healthy threshold (3) is used to determine when an unhealthy instance is considered healthy again (3 x 30 = 90 seconds of consecutive successful checks).

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to run a shell command on 50 EC2 instances simultaneously without SSH access. Which AWS service and feature should be used?
options: AWS Systems Manager Run Command
AWS Systems Manager Session Manager
AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager
AWS Systems Manager Automation
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Run Command allows executing commands on managed instances at scale without needing SSH/RDP access. It uses the SSM Agent installed on instances and supports running shell scripts, PowerShell commands, and predefined documents. Session Manager provides interactive shell sessions but is for individual instances. Patch Manager handles patching, not arbitrary commands.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to store database connection strings that include passwords for use by EC2 applications. The values must be encrypted at rest, versioned, and accessible via IAM policies. Which service should be used?
options: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store with SecureString parameters
AWS Secrets Manager
AWS Systems Manager Session Manager
Amazon S3 with server-side encryption
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Parameter Store SecureString parameters encrypt values using KMS, support versioning, and access is controlled via IAM policies. This is ideal for configuration data including connection strings. AWS Secrets Manager is also valid and adds automatic rotation, but Parameter Store is sufficient for storage and retrieval. Secrets Manager has a per-secret charge while Parameter Store standard tier is free.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to detect if the actual configuration of a CloudFormation stack has drifted from its template. Which feature should be used?
options: CloudFormation drift detection
CloudFormation change sets
AWS Config configuration recorder
CloudFormation stack policy
questions.explanation: CloudFormation drift detection compares the actual configuration of stack resources against the expected configuration defined in the template. It identifies resources that have been modified outside of CloudFormation. Change sets preview changes before execution but do not detect drift. Stack policies protect resources from being updated during stack updates but do not detect drift.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator wants to preview the impact of a CloudFormation template update before applying it to a production stack. Which feature provides this capability?
options: CloudFormation change sets
CloudFormation drift detection
CloudFormation stack policies
CloudFormation rollback triggers
questions.explanation: Change sets show you the proposed changes CloudFormation will make to your stack before execution. They detail which resources will be added, modified, or replaced, and whether the changes are disruptive (replacement). This allows review and approval before applying changes. Drift detection shows current vs expected state, not planned changes. Stack policies prevent accidental updates to specific resources.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure all EC2 instances in the organization are patched within 48 hours of a critical patch release. The solution must support multiple operating systems and provide compliance reporting. What is the BEST approach?
options: Systems Manager Patch Manager with patch baselines, maintenance windows, and compliance reporting
Custom Lambda function that SSHs into each instance and runs package updates
AWS Config rule checking for instance patch levels with manual remediation
User data scripts that run yum update on instance boot
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Patch Manager provides a fully managed patching solution. Patch baselines define which patches to approve and when (auto-approval after X days for critical patches). Maintenance windows schedule when patching occurs. Compliance reporting shows which instances are compliant. It supports Linux and Windows. Custom SSH approaches do not scale and lack compliance tracking. User data only runs at launch.

questions.question: A company wants to deploy the same CloudFormation stack to 20 AWS accounts across 4 regions in their organization. What is the MOST efficient approach?
options: CloudFormation StackSets with organization-based deployment targeting
Deploy the stack manually in each account and region
Use a CI/CD pipeline that assumes roles in each account and deploys the stack
Create an AWS Service Catalog product and share it with all accounts
questions.explanation: CloudFormation StackSets allow deploying stacks across multiple accounts and regions from a single template. With AWS Organizations integration, StackSets can automatically deploy to accounts based on OU membership. Service-managed permissions simplify the setup. This is far more efficient than manual deployment or custom pipelines for organization-wide infrastructure.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to install and configure a web server on an EC2 instance during launch. Which launch configuration feature allows running setup scripts automatically?
options: User data script
Instance metadata
Launch template tags
Instance profile
questions.explanation: EC2 user data allows specifying scripts (bash for Linux, PowerShell for Windows) that run automatically when the instance first launches. This is commonly used to install software, configure services, and download application code. Instance metadata provides information about the instance but does not execute scripts. Instance profiles assign IAM roles.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator is using CloudFormation to deploy an application stack. If the stack creation fails, the administrator wants to retain the resources that were successfully created for debugging. What should be configured?
options: Set the stack's OnFailure action to DO_NOTHING (or --disable-rollback via CLI)
Set the stack's OnFailure action to ROLLBACK and review CloudTrail logs
Enable termination protection on the stack
Add DeletionPolicy: Retain to all resources in the template
questions.explanation: Setting OnFailure to DO_NOTHING (or using --disable-rollback) prevents CloudFormation from rolling back on creation failure, leaving successfully created resources intact for debugging. The default behavior is ROLLBACK, which deletes all created resources on failure. Termination protection prevents the entire stack from being deleted but does not affect rollback behavior. DeletionPolicy: Retain prevents deletion when the stack is intentionally deleted.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to maintain consistent OS configurations across 200 EC2 instances. Configurations include installed packages, file contents, service states, and registry settings (Windows). Changes must be automatically reapplied if instances drift from the desired state. Which solution is BEST?
options: Systems Manager State Manager with association documents applied on a schedule
Systems Manager Run Command with a cron-scheduled Lambda trigger
AWS Config rules with auto-remediation for each configuration item
OpsWorks Stacks with Chef recipes applied to all instances
questions.explanation: Systems Manager State Manager ensures instances maintain a defined configuration by applying association documents on a schedule. If an instance drifts from the desired state, State Manager automatically reapplies the configuration at the next scheduled run. This handles packages, files, services, and Windows registry settings. Run Command is one-time execution without drift correction. AWS Config evaluates resource configurations but not OS-level settings.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator wants to protect a specific RDS instance in a CloudFormation stack from being accidentally replaced during a stack update. Which feature should be used?
options: CloudFormation stack policy that denies Update:Replace on the RDS resource
CloudFormation DeletionPolicy: Retain on the RDS resource
RDS deletion protection enabled on the instance
CloudFormation termination protection on the stack
questions.explanation: A stack policy is a JSON document that defines which update actions are allowed on specific resources during stack updates. Setting Deny for Update:Replace on the RDS resource prevents CloudFormation from replacing it during updates. DeletionPolicy: Retain keeps the resource when the stack is deleted but does not prevent replacement. RDS deletion protection prevents direct deletion but not CloudFormation replacement.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to automate the creation of a golden AMI that includes the latest OS patches, required software packages, and security hardening. The AMI should be rebuilt monthly. Which service is BEST suited for this?
options: EC2 Image Builder with an automated pipeline
Systems Manager Automation with a custom runbook
Lambda function that launches an instance, configures it, and creates an AMI
Packer with a cron job running on a build server
questions.explanation: EC2 Image Builder provides a fully managed service for building, testing, and distributing AMIs. You define a pipeline with build components (install packages, apply patches, run hardening scripts), test components (validate the image), and distribution settings (copy to regions/accounts). Pipelines can run on a schedule. Systems Manager Automation can build AMIs but requires more custom configuration.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to securely provide EC2 instances in a private subnet with access to Systems Manager without internet access. What is the MINIMUM set of VPC endpoints required?
options: com.amazonaws.region.ssm, com.amazonaws.region.ssmmessages, and com.amazonaws.region.ec2messages
com.amazonaws.region.ssm only
com.amazonaws.region.ssm and com.amazonaws.region.ssmmessages
A NAT gateway in a public subnet
questions.explanation: Three VPC endpoints are required for full Systems Manager functionality in a private subnet without internet access: ssm (for SSM API calls), ssmmessages (for Session Manager communication), and ec2messages (for Run Command and SSM Agent communication). Missing any one of these endpoints will cause certain SSM features to fail. A NAT gateway works but provides broader internet access than necessary.

questions.question: A CloudFormation stack update fails and automatically rolls back. The SysOps administrator needs to understand what went wrong. Where should they look FIRST?
options: CloudFormation stack events tab showing the resource that failed and the error message
CloudTrail logs for CloudFormation API calls
CloudWatch Logs for CloudFormation activity
AWS Config configuration timeline for affected resources
questions.explanation: The CloudFormation stack events tab provides a chronological list of all actions during the stack update, including which resource failed and the specific error message (e.g., 'Resource limit exceeded', 'Access denied'). This is the fastest way to identify the failure cause. CloudTrail shows API calls but not the internal CloudFormation failure details. CloudFormation does not natively log to CloudWatch Logs.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to provide a self-service catalog of pre-approved CloudFormation templates to developers. Developers should be able to launch products without having direct CloudFormation or resource-creation permissions. Which service enables this?
options: AWS Service Catalog
AWS CloudFormation StackSets
AWS Systems Manager Automation
AWS CodePipeline
questions.explanation: AWS Service Catalog allows administrators to create portfolios of approved products (CloudFormation templates) that users can launch via a self-service portal. Service Catalog uses launch constraints with IAM roles, so end users do not need direct permissions to create resources. StackSets deploy across accounts/regions but are not a self-service catalog. CodePipeline is for CI/CD.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to securely connect to an EC2 instance in a private subnet for troubleshooting. The company policy prohibits opening SSH ports in security groups and using bastion hosts. Which approach should be used?
options: AWS Systems Manager Session Manager
EC2 Instance Connect
AWS Direct Connect with a VPN
EC2 Serial Console
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Session Manager provides a secure, browser-based or CLI-based shell to EC2 instances without opening inbound ports, managing SSH keys, or using bastion hosts. It uses the SSM Agent and IAM for authentication. All sessions are logged to CloudTrail and can be logged to S3/CloudWatch Logs. EC2 Instance Connect still requires port 22 to be open in the security group.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to deploy a CloudFormation stack that creates IAM roles with custom policies. The stack deployment fails with 'Requires capabilities: CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM'. What does this error mean and how should it be resolved?
options: The template creates IAM resources with custom names; acknowledge by adding CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM when deploying
The IAM user deploying the stack lacks permissions to create IAM resources
The template references IAM resources that do not exist yet
CloudFormation cannot create IAM resources in the current region
questions.explanation: When a CloudFormation template creates IAM resources with custom names (specified Name properties), AWS requires explicit acknowledgment via the CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM capability. This is a safety measure because custom-named IAM resources could be used for privilege escalation. CAPABILITY_IAM is required for unnamed IAM resources. The capability can be specified in the CLI with --capabilities or in the console checkbox.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to deploy an application using a rolling deployment strategy that replaces instances in batches. The deployment must automatically roll back if CloudWatch alarms indicate increased error rates. Which service provides this capability?
options: AWS CodeDeploy with a deployment group configured for rolling deployment and automatic rollback on alarm
AWS Elastic Beanstalk with rolling updates
CloudFormation with AutoScalingRollingUpdate update policy
AWS CodePipeline with a manual approval stage
questions.explanation: AWS CodeDeploy supports in-place rolling deployments with configurable batch sizes and automatic rollback triggered by CloudWatch alarms. When an alarm breaches during deployment, CodeDeploy automatically reverts to the previous application revision. Elastic Beanstalk rolling updates do not support alarm-triggered rollbacks natively. CloudFormation rolling updates replace instances but lack alarm-based rollback.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to execute a multi-step automated workflow that includes stopping an EC2 instance, creating a snapshot, updating a tag, and starting the instance. Which Systems Manager feature should be used?
options: Systems Manager Automation with a runbook
Systems Manager Run Command with multiple documents
Systems Manager State Manager with an association
Systems Manager Maintenance Windows with a task
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Automation executes multi-step workflows defined in automation runbooks (documents). Steps can include API calls, Run Command execution, approval gates, and conditional branching. AWS provides pre-built runbooks for common tasks, and custom runbooks can be created. Run Command executes single commands on instances but does not orchestrate multi-step workflows across AWS services.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator has a CloudFormation template that provisions an RDS database. During an update that changes the engine version, CloudFormation wants to replace the database (delete and recreate). How can the administrator update the engine version without losing data?
options: Add UpdateReplacePolicy: Snapshot to the RDS resource so CloudFormation creates a snapshot before replacement
Use the AWS CLI to modify the RDS instance engine version directly, then update the template to match
Add DeletionPolicy: Retain to keep the old database, then manually migrate data
Perform a blue/green deployment by creating a new stack with the updated engine version
questions.explanation: UpdateReplacePolicy: Snapshot tells CloudFormation to create a snapshot of the RDS instance before deleting it during replacement. The new instance can then be restored from this snapshot. This preserves data during replacement operations. Modifying outside CloudFormation causes drift. DeletionPolicy: Retain keeps the old database but the new one would be empty.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to schedule Systems Manager Patch Manager to patch EC2 instances every Sunday at 2 AM with a maximum duration of 4 hours. Which feature facilitates this scheduling?
options: Systems Manager Maintenance Windows
EventBridge scheduled rules
CloudWatch alarm-triggered actions
Auto Scaling scheduled actions
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Maintenance Windows define a recurring schedule and duration for performing administrative tasks such as patching. You register targets (instances by tag or ID) and tasks (Patch Manager patching), and set the schedule using cron expressions. Maintenance Windows ensure patches are only applied during approved time periods and stop if the window expires.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to view a complete inventory of all software installed on managed EC2 instances across the account. Which Systems Manager feature provides this?
options: Systems Manager Inventory
Systems Manager Compliance
Systems Manager Explorer
AWS Config resource inventory
questions.explanation: Systems Manager Inventory collects metadata from managed instances including installed applications, OS patches, running services, Windows roles, network configurations, and more. This data can be queried and visualized. Compliance shows compliance status against baselines. Explorer provides an aggregated operational view. AWS Config tracks AWS resource configurations, not instance software inventory.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to import an existing VPC that was created manually into a CloudFormation stack for future management. Which CloudFormation feature enables this?
options: CloudFormation resource import
CloudFormation drift detection
CloudFormation change sets
CloudFormation custom resources
questions.explanation: CloudFormation resource import allows bringing existing resources into CloudFormation management without recreating them. You provide the resource identifier (VPC ID) and a template that describes the resource. After import, CloudFormation manages the resource as part of the stack. Drift detection checks for differences but does not import resources. This feature avoids the need to tear down and recreate existing infrastructure.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to implement a blue/green deployment for an application running on EC2 instances behind an ALB. The solution must allow testing the new version before switching traffic and enable instant rollback. Which approach is BEST?
options: Create a new target group with instances running the new version, use ALB weighted target group routing to shift traffic gradually
Deploy a second Auto Scaling group, manually update the ALB listener to point to the new target group
Use Route 53 weighted routing to split traffic between two ALBs
Deploy new instances in the existing Auto Scaling group and remove old instances one by one
questions.explanation: ALB supports weighted target group routing in listener rules, allowing you to send a percentage of traffic to a new target group (green) while the existing target group (blue) handles the rest. You can test with 1% traffic, gradually increase, and instantly roll back by setting the blue target group weight back to 100%. Route 53-based routing adds DNS propagation delays. Manually updating listeners is not instant.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to distribute a configuration file to 100 EC2 instances and ensure the file remains consistent over time. If any instance's file changes, it should be automatically reverted. Which approach is MOST reliable?
options: Systems Manager State Manager association using an AWS-RunDocument that applies the configuration on a schedule
Systems Manager Run Command to distribute the file once
S3 bucket with the configuration file and a cron job on each instance to download it
CloudFormation cfn-init with a template that specifies the file contents
questions.explanation: State Manager associations apply configurations on a recurring schedule. If an instance's file is manually changed, State Manager automatically reapplies the correct configuration at the next scheduled run, enforcing consistency. Run Command is one-time and does not auto-correct drift. S3 with cron requires custom tooling on each instance. cfn-init only runs during stack operations, not continuously.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator has a CloudFormation template that references a Lambda function code zip in S3. The code changes frequently but the S3 object key stays the same. After updating the code in S3, updating the stack does not deploy the new code. Why?
options: CloudFormation detects no change in the template because the S3 key has not changed
Lambda functions cannot be updated through CloudFormation
The Lambda function's execution role lacks S3 access
CloudFormation caches Lambda code and does not re-download it
questions.explanation: CloudFormation only detects changes by comparing template properties. If the S3 bucket and key remain the same, CloudFormation sees no change and does not update the Lambda function. The solution is to include an S3ObjectVersion property or change the S3 key (e.g., include a version hash in the filename). This forces CloudFormation to detect a change and update the function code.

questions.question: A company is migrating from on-premises Chef-managed infrastructure to AWS. They want to continue using existing Chef cookbooks for configuration management. Which AWS service should the SysOps administrator recommend?
options: AWS OpsWorks for Chef Automate
AWS Systems Manager with custom documents
AWS CloudFormation with cfn-init
Amazon EC2 with Chef client installed manually
questions.explanation: AWS OpsWorks for Chef Automate provides a fully managed Chef server. Existing Chef cookbooks can be used without modification. It handles server maintenance, backups, and software updates. Systems Manager could replicate some functionality but would require rewriting cookbooks as SSM documents. Manual Chef installation requires managing the Chef server infrastructure. OpsWorks is the natural migration path for existing Chef users.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure that only approved AMIs can be used to launch EC2 instances in the organization. Which approach enforces this across all accounts?
options: A Service Control Policy (SCP) that denies ec2:RunInstances unless the AMI matches approved image IDs
An AWS Config rule that checks running instances against approved AMIs
An IAM policy attached to all users that restricts AMI usage
EC2 Image Builder with distribution settings limited to approved accounts
questions.explanation: An SCP with a condition key (ec2:ImageId) can deny ec2:RunInstances unless the AMI is in an approved list. SCPs apply to all accounts in the organization, enforcing the policy centrally. AWS Config can detect non-compliant instances after launch but does not prevent launch. IAM policies would need to be applied to every principal in every account. Image Builder creates AMIs but does not control which AMIs can be used.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to grant an EC2 instance permission to access an S3 bucket without storing AWS credentials on the instance. What should be configured?
options: An IAM role attached to the instance via an instance profile
An IAM user with access keys stored in environment variables
An S3 bucket policy that allows access from the instance's IP address
AWS STS temporary credentials stored in the application configuration
questions.explanation: An IAM role attached via an instance profile provides temporary credentials that are automatically rotated. Applications using the AWS SDK automatically retrieve credentials from the instance metadata service. This is the recommended approach as it avoids storing long-term credentials. Access keys are long-term and pose a security risk if exposed. S3 bucket policies based on IP are fragile when IPs change.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator discovers that an IAM user has both an inline policy allowing s3:* on all resources and a managed policy denying s3:DeleteBucket. The user attempts to delete an S3 bucket. What is the result?
options: The request is denied because an explicit deny always overrides an allow
The request is allowed because the inline policy takes precedence over managed policies
The request is allowed because the allow policy is more specific
The request results in an error because of conflicting policies
questions.explanation: In IAM policy evaluation, an explicit Deny ALWAYS overrides any Allow, regardless of whether the policies are inline or managed. This is a fundamental IAM principle. If any policy attached to the principal contains an explicit Deny for the action, the request is denied. The evaluation order is: explicit deny > explicit allow > implicit deny (default).

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure all data stored on EBS volumes is encrypted. What is the SIMPLEST way to enforce this across all new EBS volumes in a region?
options: Enable EBS encryption by default in the EC2 settings for the region
Create a custom AWS Config rule to check for unencrypted volumes
Add a condition key to the IAM policy denying unencrypted volume creation
Use a Lambda function triggered by CloudTrail to encrypt new volumes
questions.explanation: Enabling EBS encryption by default in the AWS account settings (per region) ensures all new EBS volumes and snapshots are automatically encrypted without requiring users to specify encryption at creation time. This is the simplest approach as it requires no policy management. AWS Config can detect but not prevent. IAM conditions work but require policy management across all principals.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to rotate a KMS customer-managed key used to encrypt S3 objects. What happens to existing encrypted objects when automatic key rotation is enabled?
options: Existing objects remain encrypted with the old key material; KMS transparently decrypts them using the original key material stored in the key's history
All existing objects are automatically re-encrypted with the new key material
Existing objects become inaccessible until manually re-encrypted
S3 automatically creates new copies of objects with the new key material
questions.explanation: When KMS automatic key rotation is enabled, new key material is generated annually. The CMK ID remains the same, and KMS retains all previous key material versions. Existing objects remain encrypted with their original key material. When accessed, KMS transparently uses the correct key material version for decryption. No re-encryption is needed. The rotation is transparent to applications.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to grant a third-party AWS account temporary access to an S3 bucket in the company's account. The access should be limited to 1 hour and should not require creating IAM users in either account. What is the BEST approach?
options: Create an IAM role with a trust policy allowing the third-party account to assume it, with a maximum session duration of 1 hour
Generate a pre-signed URL for the S3 objects with a 1-hour expiration
Add the third-party account ID to the S3 bucket policy
Create an IAM user with temporary access keys that expire after 1 hour
questions.explanation: An IAM cross-account role with a trust policy is the recommended approach for granting temporary cross-account access. The third-party account assumes the role and receives temporary credentials limited by the session duration. Pre-signed URLs work for individual objects but not for general bucket access. Bucket policies with account IDs provide permanent access, not temporary. IAM access keys do not have built-in expiration.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to ensure that all S3 buckets in the organization have server-side encryption enabled and that public access is blocked. Which approach provides continuous enforcement with the LEAST effort?
options: AWS Config managed rules s3-bucket-server-side-encryption-enabled and s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited with auto-remediation
S3 Storage Lens with recommendations dashboard
Manual quarterly audits using the S3 console
Lambda function triggered by S3 CreateBucket events
questions.explanation: AWS Config managed rules continuously evaluate S3 bucket configurations. s3-bucket-server-side-encryption-enabled checks encryption and s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited checks public access. Auto-remediation using SSM Automation can automatically fix non-compliant buckets. This provides continuous, automated compliance enforcement. S3 Storage Lens provides metrics but not enforcement. Lambda on CreateBucket misses configuration changes to existing buckets.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator needs to restrict all IAM users and roles in child accounts from creating resources outside of the us-east-1 and eu-west-1 regions. What should be used?
options: A Service Control Policy (SCP) with a region restriction condition
IAM policies attached to all users denying non-approved regions
AWS Config rules checking resource region compliance
VPC configurations limited to approved regions
questions.explanation: SCPs set the maximum permissions boundary for all principals in an account. An SCP with an aws:RequestedRegion condition key can deny all actions except in approved regions. This applies organization-wide without modifying individual IAM policies. IAM policies would need to be attached to every principal in every account. AWS Config detects but does not prevent resource creation.

questions.question: A SysOps administrator discovers that Security Hub has flagged several findings related to public S3 buckets, unencrypted RDS instances, and overly permissive security groups. The administrator needs to prioritize remediation. According to Security Hub, how are findings prioritized?
options: Findings are assigned a severity label (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFORMATIONAL) and a normalized severity score
Findings are ranked by AWS account number and resource type
Findings are prioritized based on the compliance standard they violate
All findings have equal priority and must be addressed simultaneously
questions.explanation: Security Hub assigns severity labels and normalized scores (0-100) to findings based on the potential security impact. CRITICAL findings (score 90-100) should be addressed first (e.g., public S3 buckets with sensitive data), followed by HIGH (70-89), MEDIUM (40-69), LOW (1-39), and INFORMATIONAL (0). This helps administrators prioritize remediation based on risk.
